title: Theatre Yermy and Yahir — Oct 2020 college-app todos
html:
TL;DR

  • 17yo Yahir stacks five todos at the start of his senior-year college push: AP Micro Econ, college application, October scholarships, FAFSA, lentils-and-peas (grocery)
  • "Theatre Yermy and Yahir" header — Yahir and Emilio (Yermy) are doing high-school theatre together in fall 2020; rare evidence of brothers sharing a hobby in the corpus
  • Self-prompt at the bottom: "Easiest way to apply to colleges, college app and coalition? Directly through school?" — Yahir reasoning about application platforms in real-time
  • One of the earliest FAFSA mentions, ~6 months before the April 2021 Ford OSU prep makes financial-aid the central anxiety
  • Lentils-and-peas grocery item embedded in a college-app todo list is peak genre-mixing

Notes

Two threads in five lines. Thread one: Yahir's college-application machinery is starting to spin up — AP Micro Econ (course rigor), Common App vs. Coalition platform decision, FAFSA, October scholarships. The platform question ("Easiest way to apply to colleges, college app and coalition? Directly through school?") shows Yahir doing the meta-research without a college counselor visible in the note — the small town high school kid figuring out the application market alone.

Thread two: brothers in theatre. "Theatre Yermy and Yahir" is the title-line, which means the note's primary association is the after-school activity, not the college apps — those slot in underneath the theatre context. Emilio would be ~14 in fall 2020, Yahir 17. This is one of very few notes that documents them in a shared school activity (vs. the more typical Yahir-as-Emilio-protector framing). It expands Emilio's densified profile: the brothers weren't just family-call partners later — they had a shared high-school stage moment.

The lentils-and-peas line is the thesis: the grocery list and the FAFSA live in the same notebook, on the same page, with no hierarchy between them.

Connections

emilio · yahir family · fafsa · common app · coalition app · ford family foundation · osu · grants pass teen era · covid pandemic era · 2020 11 15 scholarship essays todo
